theme: grocery app schedules a year of coupons
title: Loyalty Ledger: A Year of Grocery-App Coupons
strategies: always give the biggest coupon every week (never touches the pilot data; reference price converges to the discounted price almost immediately, permanently thinning margin)

set-and-forget: solve a one-shot profit-maximizing constant discount using a generic assumed elasticity, then apply that same depth every week all year (ignores hidden per-segment elasticity and the erosion the constant policy itself causes)

identify each segment's hidden memory constant and price sensitivity from the closed-form pilot data, decide whether the segment is worth discounting at all, then ration the discount as short recovery-spaced pulses tuned via a local replay of the reference-price law
invalid: submit a schedule with discount depths above max_discount on every segment/week, failing validation
